data.title: Intuitive Surgical Q3: Da Vinci 5 Robot Set For 2025, But Overvalued
data.description:
I initiated a 'Sell' rating on Intuitive Surgical (NASDAQ:ISRG) (NEOE:ISRG:CA) in January 2024. The company plans to roll out their Da Vinci 5 robot in mid-2025, which could be a major catalyst for their stock price. While I favor their strong leadership in the robotic assisted, minimally invasive surgery market, I am concerned about their hefty valuation. I reiterate a 'Sell' rating with a fair value of $350 per share.

Rollout Da Vinci 5 Robot in 2025

In March 2024, Intuitive Surgical announced that the FDA has provided 510k clearance for Da Vinci 5, the company's next-generation multiport robotic system. Da Vinci 5 is designed to improve accuracy and precision with next-generation 3D display and imaging systems. According to the management, surgeons feedback on da Vinci 5 highlighted improvement in precision, imaging and ergonomics.

Intuitive Surgical has been working on the hardware and software update to Da Vinci 5 in the second half of 2024. At the same time, the company is expanding their manufacturing capabilities for the new products. As a result, Intuitive Surgical plans to roll out the new Da Vinci 5 robot in mid-2025. I view the global rollout of Da Vinci 5 as a major catalyst for the stock price in 2025, as Intuitive Surgical is poised to benefit from the instrument replacement and trade-in opportunities.

During the earnings call, the management indicated that the company has installed 188 Da Vinci 5 systems with over 12,000 procedures. I am impressed by their R&D and marketing progress for Da Vinci 5.

Q3 and Outlook

Intuitive Surgical released their Q3 result on October 17th after the market closes, reporting 16.9% revenue growth and 26% adjusted EPS growth. The standout achievement during the quarter was they placed 379 da Vinci surgical systems, growing by 21.5% year-over-year.

OliverWyman predicts that robotic-assisted surgery market could hit $14 billion globally by 2026, with a CAGR of nearly 11%. As shown in the chart below, the robotic-assisted surgery could be utilized for minimally invasive procedures across gynaecological, urological, neuro and orthopedic surgeries.

I anticipate Intuitive Surgical will deliver 15% organic revenue growth in the near future, and key growth drivers include:

  • The Da Vinci 5 launch is likely to drive significant growth for Intuitive Surgical, considering their innovative technology and positive feedback from surgeons. In addition, Da Vinci 5 carries a higher portion of software components, which could potentially allow Intuitive Surgical to integrate AI and connected technology in operation rooms.
  • While Medtronic (MDT) and Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) are investing in robotic-assisted surgery, Intuitive Surgical continues to lead the market. The introduction of Da Vinci 5 will further solidify the company's leading position in the industry. Given the lengthy R&D cycles and FDA approval processes required for surgical robots, it would be hard for other players to catch up to Intuitive Surgical.
  • Intuitive Surgical has been growing their international markets, which now represent 34% of total revenue. I think Intuitive Surgical has a huge growth potential in these international markets, while it takes time to train surgeons and nurses on the new systems.

Therefore, I anticipate Intuitive Surgical will grow their revenue by 15% annually, comprising 11% market growth and 4% share gains. Over the earnings call, the management disclosed that the total number of procedures performed with their multi-port products has grown at a CAGR of 17% over the past five years. Compared to the historical growth, my assumption for their future organic revenue growth is relatively conservative.

During the earnings call, the management pointed out the lower component and logistic costs would be margin accretive. I model 40bps annual margin expansion due to the operating leverage. I calculate that the company will grow their operating expenses by 12.5% annually, leading to 40bps margin expansion.

The WACC is calculated to be 13% assuming: risk free rate 3.6%; beta 1.38; cost of debt 5%; equity risk premium 7%; equity $172 billion; debt $0; tax rate 23%. The DCF is summarized as follows:

The fair value is calculated to be $350 per share after discounting all the future FCF, according to my DCF model.

Key Risks

As I assign a 'Sell' rating to Intuitive Surgical, I am considering the following upside risks:

  • Intuitive Surgical exited the quarter with $8.3 billion in cash and cash equivalents without any debt, up from $7.7 billion in the previous quarter. As such, the company has built up a huge amount of cash balance thanks to their strong free cash flow generations. The company has the capacity to repurchase their own stocks, which could push their stock price higher in the future.
  • I believe Intuitive Surgical has begun to establish their software business. If the company can successfully monetize their software and service offerings, it could create a more recurring and stable revenue stream in the future.

Conclusion

It's impressive to see Intuitive Surgical is set to roll out the new Da Vinci 5 robots in 2025. While I am confident that Intuitive Surgical will continue to lead the competition, I believe the stock price is currently overvalued. I reiterate a 'Sell' rating with a fair value of $350 per share.

data.title: Gold And The Dow Both Trade At All-Time Highs: What History Says
data.description:
Over the past month we have again experienced both the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) (DIA) and U.S. price for Gold (GLD) (IAU) (GLDM) (SGOL) reaching for all-time highs. Believe it or not, this dual move is incredibly rare historically. Yet, it has been a regular occurrence during all of 2024.

Since the early 1960s, record highs for both have happened at roughly the same moment (within three months for a time frame) only a handful of instances. Below I have charted zigzags in each asset over the last 64 years. You will notice just early 1973, late 2007, and August-November 2020 have produced dual highs under my definition (highlighted with green arrows).

Let's review what happened to both assets for price direction shortly after dual records were reached.

1973

Gold priced in U.S. dollars traded in Europe did break above the official government mandated gold-tie to our currency at $35 an ounce in the late 1960s. Prices closer to $50 in the free market caused many nations to cash in paper dollars for physical gold at the Fed bank in New York, an effort to own and resell the metal overseas. This fact was one of the reasons the U.S. government under President Nixon decided to leave a gold standard (convertibility for foreign central banks).

The argument was a fixed currency system was antiquated, with free markets for this important monetary metal a better long-term idea. At the time, two of the main proponents for leaving a gold standard were Alan Greenspan and Paul Volcker, Fed chairmen in waiting.

Well, with the Dow setting an all-time high a little over 1,000 in January 1966, investors had to wait until the end of 1972 for another break (temporarily) to new records (circled in green below). Gold prices had reached a new high of $65 in 1972 and continued to spike after February 1973 (also circled in green).

You can review the new "dual high" signal on the second chart, with performance calculations using the same chart scale thereafter. An oil shock crisis by OPEC and skyrocketing grain/farm prices would be next. Gold loved the implosion in the U.S. dollar's value internationally, while stocks and the economy did not. A major manufacturing and consumer spending recession followed into 1974.

2007

It would take a good 34 years before the next concurrent all-time high situation unfolded. Ultra-low short-term interest rates after the Dotcom Technology Bust of 2000-02 (artificially suppressed by the Federal Reserve) would lay the groundwork for a major buying spree in gold by investors the world over. The extra liquidity also created a real estate bubble in America.

Wall Street didn't mind the excess liquidity in the financial system and rose to new record pricing in 2007 (circled in green below). Unfortunately, a massive real estate bust was approaching, with a Great Financial Crisis for the U.S. banking system just around the corner.

A few months later, all-time highs for gold were breached above the 1980 high of $800 (circled in green). Old-time investors, central banks, and spreading fear in the financial markets would initially create the new gold highs. Yet, the extraordinary money printing reaction (introduction of QE support for Treasury deficit spending by then Fed Chairman Bernanke) to the Great Recession, including unprecedented bank bailouts, would only serve to reinforce gold demand by the masses.

Depending on how you count closing or intraday highs for gold from early 1980, new records for the world's main monetary metal were reached days to weeks after the final Dow Industrial high in October 2007.

2020

The COVID pandemic of 2020 created all kinds of unique trading results, first from fear and panic, then from the greatest level of monetary expansion since the 1860s Civil War. Gold would be expected to get a bid from both outcomes, and it did. All-time highs were reached in August 2020, quickly followed by new Dow Industrial records in early November.

And, I know Wall Street financial experts don't want to admit it, but gold has performed just as well as stocks since the beginning of the pandemic. The good news for regular equity investors is money printing and government supports for banks and the economy have remained in place.

Somehow, we have yet to experience a recession, despite far higher interest rates and a dreaded inverted Treasury yield curve since the end of 2022. The open question is - will our luck run out?

As my second chart below illustrates, we have been outlining new highs in the Dow Industrials and gold concurrently pretty much all of 2024. In terms of a warning signal about the future for the U.S. financial markets and economy, gold's recent trading action is about the loudest scream for caution, maybe ever in my trading career (since 1986).

Conclusions

The best explanation of the signal sent by the twin move to new record highs is the "smart" money (experienced traders, hedge funds, rich investors, central banks) may be running to gold to hedge wealth, at the same moment as the trend-following (FOMO), fast money crowd is rushing into the equity market. Eventually, most retail investors end up transitioning capital into gold as they sell stocks on economic/political turmoil in the United States.

While the late 2020 to early 2021 experience had limited success in predicting both a major move higher in gold and turn lower in stocks, the 1973-74 and 2007-09 recession patterns could still repeat. The preponderance of statistical evidence (greater than 50% of the time) suggests holding lighter-than-normal stock market weightings (without even considering the extended valuation problem for U.S. stock returns in late 2024), matched against increased gold exposure in your portfolio.

This has been my portfolio design all of 2024 actually, and I see little reason to change it. The major surprise for markets soon could be a jump in crude oil and energy prices into 2025 on a Middle East turmoil supply shock. If this takes place, 4% or 5% CPI inflation will be the new normal, with a substantial up-move in long-term interest rates almost surely hurting the economy and equity pricing.

Then, add on the likelihood of higher tax rates to cut out-of-control deficit spending by Uncle Sam, and perhaps greater tariffs on imported goods (if Trump wins the November election), and it's easy to worry about a stagflation outcome for the economy next year.

Under this scenario, stocks may decline sharply in value, while hard assets like gold (and its monetary hedge features) will continue to witness heavy accumulation to protect wealth from the ravages of inflation and dollar devaluations. It all makes sense, really.

Interestingly, 1973 and 2007 were followed by severe recessions approximately a year later. Will 2025 produce the same? In addition, 1974 and 2008 each experienced spikes in crude oil pricing (as did 2022, not long after the November 2020 dual-high signal).

However you slice it, when both the Dow Jones Industrials and U.S. gold price are reaching new record highs in tandem, economic risks appear to be quite elevated, stock market quotes seem to be at greater risk than usual, while inflows of capital into gold look like a good bet in future years.

If you are searching for gold/silver/platinum investments to own over the next year or two, I have written on a significant number of picks since the summer of 2023. You can scroll through the articles under my name to hunt for the ideas that suit your interests and risk appetite best.

Thanks for reading. Please consider this article a first step in your due diligence process. Consulting with a registered and experienced investment advisor is recommended before making any trade.

data.title: SL Green: Break Time As Part Of A Fantastic Year
data.description:
SL Green (NYSE:SLG) announced that they leased 925,000 sq ft that was a combination of a lease renewal and leasing additional space to Bloomberg. At the same time, the company announced that it was on course to lease more than 3,000,000 sq ft to get occupancy up to 92.5% by the end of the fiscal year. This happened one quarter after the company raised the free cash flow estimate that was part of the last article and the second quarter earnings announcement. This management has far outperformed the industry, even with the currently relatively quiet quarterly report. This stock, which has more than doubled from its bottom point, has more to go.

During the conference call, management reviewed some of the fiscal year's accomplishments. Today's announcement of a major new lease would seem to imply that the leasing activities have maintained a brisk pace.

It was noted that the bottom of the market was considered a year ago. Ever since then, this management has gone on to justify its optimism by meeting a rather ambitious strategic plan for the year that the market initially gave management little hope of achieving.

Stock Price Action

The result of management "outperforming" in the eyes of the market has been a steady increase in the stock price.

The stock price has definitely responded to the third quarter report and the conference call as well. If you believe that management has used the past year to continue adding value to the portfolio through rehabilitation projects while also selling non-core items, then the stock price is likely to go higher in the coming fiscal year. There is also likely to be more cash to distribute to shareholders in the future as well.

What I stated (repeatedly) before the fiscal year began, is that with such a dour market view, things could only get better. I lined myself up firmly with management because I believed that they knew their business better than the market did. That is often the case with management that outperforms.

What needs to be said is that management needs to be thoroughly evaluated by the investor before making an investment. That is always the hardest part of the investment decision. Because it is often so hard, this is the step that Mr. Market often skips (or puts little weight on). But yet management evaluation is often the key to investment success.

Some would argue that at this point the "easy money" has been made. By the time all the "buy ratings" come out, most of the gains are already in the stock price. That is certainly possible.

I, personally, look at this as a growth situation. Management has had considerable success purchasing old buildings and modernizing them to the point that the money received increases significantly. I like their chances of continuing that in the future.

Furthermore, there is now a significant new business of managing assets for other entities. This gives management access to unnatural owner which could be a means of finding some bargain purchases for a profitable rehab project.

Earnings

One of the things to note is that the key ratios on the summary statement below continue to improve.

The funds flow from operations are down in the current quarter (compared to the second quarter) because there are no gains from sales in the current quarter. Management still has things that they want to sell, but that does not mean gains (or ever larger gains) will appear every quarter so that comparisons are "smooth".

In fact, sales are way ahead of the expectations of Mr. Market. Clearly, the stock price at the beginning of the fiscal year expected no sales. Now, going forward, with a considerably higher stock price, future expectations are definitely higher.

In the meantime, the raised expectations for funds flow for the fiscal year have been maintained. It was also mentioned that the occupancy rate should climb significantly by the end of the fiscal year. This is happening at a time when many managements are reporting less than stellar years for the REIT industry as a whole.

Management Attitude

Probably the biggest cause of initial market doubt was that management stated that the correct price given market conditions was the most important part of the strategic plan. So far, that attitude has been proven to be correct, as this management has accomplished so much that the stock price has roughly doubled from the low price achieved in the previous fiscal year. It is still something that is being emphasized (one way or another) throughout the conference call.

But the ability to achieve guidance at a time when the market gave management no chance is a sign of superior management because excellent management usually surprises to the upside. Clearly, Mr. Market has been pleasantly surprised the whole fiscal year.

Summary

SL Green is a stock that is leading the office REIT recovery at a time when it is "common knowledge" that office space supply is far past office demand. Therefore, everyone "knows" that office REIT's will be struggling for the foreseeable future.

But this is a company that found a niche in the New York City market that beats the common knowledge. There is every chance that this management will continue to outperform in the future. That means that SL Green remains a strong buy.

Whenever I find a management with a tendency to outperform market expectations, I tend to hold until the story changes. For SL Green, that could be a very long time to come. Some may want to sell when the stock becomes adequately priced. But given the market expectations about the office REIT stocks in general, it could be a while before this sector returns to favor.

Stocks tend to climb "a wall of worry". This wall has a whole lot of worries. As long as this stock continues to beat those worries and gain favor in the market, I intend to hold unless the price becomes far out of line in terms of valuation.

Risks

The CEO position is open right now (the Chairman of the Board is interim CEO). That could prove problematic if it persists too long.

The loss of services of key personnel can be far more costly for a management like this one that outperforms. The number of superior managers is a subset of the number of managers that qualify for the position.

The New York City market is cyclical like any other large city. Downturns in the market often do not give a lot of warning. Therefore, a cyclical downturn can derail even the best plans of a good management.

It should also be noted that the company is not geographically diversified. It is therefore very dependent upon the fortunes of New York City.

data.title: Flowserve: More Room To Run Despite The Stock's Recent Rally
data.description:
Flowserve Corporation (NYSE:FLS) has recently been in the spotlight after announcing they've completed the acquisition of MOAS Industries.

With Q3 2024 earnings in just a few weeks, investors are wondering if the strong momentum Flowserve has shown over the past year will keep going.

In my view, the answer is yes, despite some risks and challenges that I will cover in this article.

In the outlook section, I will deep dive into the fundamentals of the company, including an overview of insider and activist investor activity. Additionally, I will analyze the company's valuation ratios, and provide my arguments on why I believe the company is not overvalued, despite the recent bull run.

For now, I would like to start with a brief company overview section, for those readers new to this stock.

Company Overview

Flowserve Corporation is a manufacturer of flow control products such as pumps, valves, and seals. Their primary customers include the oil and gas, chemical, power generation, and water industries.

They generate revenue through equipment sales and also through aftermarket services, including maintenance, repair, replacement parts, and diagnostic services.

The company operates in two business segments:

  • The Flowserve Pumps Division (FPD): this segment is focused on the design and manufacturing of both custom and standard pumps, as well as mechanical seals and systems.
  • The Flow Control Division (FCD): this segment is focused on producing specialized isolation and control valves and valve automation products.

I considered including below a breakdown of the revenue per business segment from the latest 10-Q.

Ownership-wise, I remain disappointed with management's low skin in the game. The latest 14A shows less than 1% of total beneficial ownership among all 15 directors and executive officers.

As shown below, institutional investors own 96% of the common stock.

Headwinds And Challenges

I'd like to start with dessert.

In Q2 2024, operating cash flow declined by 153% YoY.

Furthermore, the decline in free cash flow was even more pronounced, with a 480% YoY drop.

Considering that the company experienced a 7% revenue growth during the quarter, this raised some eyebrows. As a matter of fact, just a few days after Q2 earnings release, the share price was down by 15%. So, what happened?

If we dive deep into the cash flow statement, you will notice an unfavorable increase in accounts receivable and accrued expenses.

However, I am not overly concerned about this increase. To be specific, the $168.5 million increase in accounts receivable is linked to project-related billings and milestone completions.

Additionally, during the Q2 conference call, management expressed confidence in cash flow generation during the second part of the year, projecting full-year cash flow conversion to reach approximately 80% or more of adjusted net income.

Moving on, the company seems to be involved in a class-action lawsuit related to employee rights, which was scheduled for a final approval hearing on October 17, 2024. In my view, this is not something that can be overlooked, although considering the size of the company (16,000 employees), unfortunately, these types of lawsuits are common across big corporations.

Finally, I need to highlight the geopolitical risk due to their high market exposure in the Middle East. I considered including below a breakdown of revenue per geographical area.

As seen above, 16% of the total revenue of the company comes from the Middle East and Africa region.

In my view, there is a risk in this region due to the current conflict between Israel and Hamas.

Outlook

I'd like to start with price action.

A quick look at the daily chart below shows a 125% increase over the last 24 months.

From a technical analysis perspective, there could be a potential resistance between $55 and $60. However, there are many reasons (that I discuss below) that make me believe the share price will break past this resistance level.

A quick look at the income statement shows a significant growth in revenue since Q4 2022.

However, this increase in revenue was also accompanied by an increase in profitability. Operating and net income followed this increase, with an exceptional growth rate in the last 2 quarters.

Additionally, with the recent acquisition of MOGAS Industries (for $305 million), the company is strengthening its aftermarket business, which has consistently been a high-margin sector. Therefore, I expect these figures to improve in the next few quarters.

The balance sheet looks healthy, with net debt declining 11% YoY.

Additionally, the liquidity ratios look quite decent.

Ratio Value
Current Ratio 2.00
Quick Ratio 1.40
Cash Ratio 0.37

In regard to their debt structure, it primarily consists of senior notes, a term loan, and a revolving credit facility.

The nearest senior note to expiration is in 2030 ($495.8 million at 3.5%). The term loan is less favorable, with a 6.68% interest rate. It's scheduled to mature on September 13, 2026, with $30 million of principal repayments due in the remainder of 2024, and $60 million in 2025.

Finally, Flowserve has an $800 million unsecured revolving credit facility, at a 6.8% interest rate, of which $75 million was drawn in Q2 2024.

I've already discussed the cash flow statement in the risk section above, so I will not mention it again here.

Moving on to insider activity, I remain disappointed with the lack of insider buying activity this year. Furthermore, there were 2 selling operations by key executives, one of the CEO. To be specific, Rowe Robert Scott, the CEO, sold 22% of his ownership in the company for $5 million, in May this year.

This was already 5 months ago, so I am not overly concerned considering the momentum of the bull run. However, if he was to sell now, then I would begin to sweat (considerably). Therefore, I recommend keeping a close eye on insider activity.

There was no recent activist buying activity; however, it seems that institutional investors are starting to lock in profits, with a 40% increase in Q2 2024 in reduced positions. Additionally, it seems that the put/call ratio has increased substantially (117%) in the second quarter, signaling some pessimism among institutional investors.

And, looking at the valuation metrics below, I understand why this is the case.

P/B and P/S are trading close to their 5-year maximum. However, I am not overly concerned about these 2 ratios. If we compare the company with some of its peers, and we sort the comparison table by P/B in descending order, the company is in the lower half of the table. Therefore, I believe that P/B and P/S do not indicate overvaluation.

Conclusion

To wrap up, here is why I maintain a Buy rating.

The company has shown impressive revenue and profitability growth over the last 24 months, with a consistent double-digit growth in operating income since Q2 2022. I believe this trend could continue in the next quarters, especially after acquiring MOGAS Industries and its high-margin aftermarket business.

The balance sheet looks healthy, with declining net debt and strong liquidity ratios. Additionally, management's projection of improved cash flow by the end of the year also gives me some added confidence.

However, there are some concerns that cannot be overlooked. One of them is the sharp decline in operating and free cash flow in Q2 2024 which raised some eyebrows, even though it appears to be linked to milestone-related payment agreements. Additionally, the company's significant exposure to the Middle East presents geopolitical risks, particularly with ongoing regional conflicts.

To conclude, I believe the current momentum is bound to continue into the next quarters. Therefore, I maintain a Buy rating for this stock.

data.title: Politics And The Markets 10/18/24
data.description:
This is the forum for daily political discussion on Seeking Alpha. A new version is published every market day.

Please don't leave political comments on other articles or posts on the site.

The comments below are not regulated with the same rigor as the rest of the site, and this is an 'enter at your own risk' area as discussion can get very heated. If you can't stand the heat... you know what they say...

More on Today's Markets:

Moderation Guidelines:

We remove comments under the following categories:

  • Personal attacks on another user account
  • Anti-Vaxxer or covid related misinformation
  • Stereotyping, prejudiced or racist language about individuals or the topic under discussion.
  • Inciting violence messages, encouraging hate groups and political violence.

Regardless of which side of the political divide you find yourself, please be courteous and don't direct abuse at other users.

For any issue with regards to comments please email us at : moderation@seekingalpha.com.

Seeking Alpha's Disclosure: Past performance is no guarantee of future results. No recommendation or advice is being given as to whether any investment is suitable for a particular investor. Any views or opinions expressed above may not reflect those of Seeking Alpha as a whole. Seeking Alpha is not a licensed securities dealer, broker or US investment adviser or investment bank. Our analysts are third party authors that include both professional investors and individual investors who may not be licensed or certified by any institute or regulatory body.

data.title: Webtoon: The Most Undiscovered User Attention Opportunity On The Market
data.description:
Capturing user attention is perhaps one of the most valuable things that businesses can do nowadays.

Whether you're providing entertainment products that people will pay for, like Netflix (NFLX) and Disney (DIS), or acting as a platform for advertisers like Google (GOOG), Meta (META) and TikTok (BDNCE), user attention is one of the most monetizable assets in the world.

Why? Because if you have people's attention, you can sell them things.

Consequently, companies that don't have customer's attention - which is most of them - will pay through the nose to get it.

At the same time, perhaps somewhat paradoxically, traditional media companies have struggled in the modern world.

The decline of print media, traditional advertising agencies, music labels, and film studios is well documented, as user habits have increasingly moved to social media and other digital formats like Reddit (RDDT).

One business model that has seen tremendous success is the 'Spotify' (SPOT) format, where a platform comes into a medium and builds a digital marketplace, incentivizing users to create content via revenue share. From there, it's a virtuous cycle where more and more users and creators meet in the middle and the best content rises to the top.

YouTube and Roblox (RBLX) are examples of this in the video and gaming mediums. Seeking Alpha is also an example of this in the financial media space.

Building a marketplace is hard, but the resulting network effect can be durable and highly rewarding for shareholders.

In this article, we're taking a look at WEBTOON (NASDAQ:WBTN), an attention marketplace in the anime and storytelling space. The platform boasts approximately ~170 million monthly active users that are highly engaged, and we see a lot of room for the company to grow readership and profitability as it continues to expand internationally from its roots in Asia.

Today, we'll be looking at WBTN in a lot more depth, exploring the company's financials and valuation, and explaining why we're so bullish on this mostly undiscovered user attention asset.

Sound good? Let's dive in.

WBTN's Business

WBTN is actually a recent IPO, and as a result, the company's public track record isn't as robust as normal companies that we look at.

However, we'll do our best to break it down for you.

On the business side, WBTN is interesting because we see a lot of potential, but earnings up to this point have been relatively meager. In many ways, it reminds us of Reddit, albeit at a smaller scale.

As far as inputs go, we think the business has a lot of the correct 'raw materials' for success.

First off, as we mentioned, WBTN has an enormous, highly engaged user base, which is no mean feat in today's competitive attention economy.

Between WBTN's media platforms, which include WEBTOON, NAVER WEBTOON, LINE MANGA, ebookjapan, Wattpad, Munpia, NAVER SERIES, and Wattpad WEBTOON Studios, the company boasts a massive, highly engaged user base:

With a massive selection of content to choose from, user choice isn't an issue within the platform, and with a huge user and creator base, we'd argue the company has successfully gotten this flywheel 'up and running'.

At the same time, users also appear to be highly engaged, with web comic sessions in the half-hour range, and web-novel sessions close to the hour mark. These are great numbers when it comes to time spent on-platform.

At the same time, Webtoon has a number of advantages over normal social media, in that a huge majority of users say the company's web-comic and web-novel format is entertaining and makes them feel good:

Some of this is down to the company's iconic bite-sized serialization, which brings users back week after week, and some of this is down to the fact that WBTN's content is focused on storytelling, which is in contrast with traditional social media that can often invite negative internal comparisons vs. the rich and famous.

On the whole, the user base is young - mostly between 13–34, which is a large, digital-native cohort with growing purchasing power.

Overall, we think WBTN's engaged audience and content proposition is strong and has the potential to grow and monetize well over the long term.

WBTN's Financials

When it comes to translating the engaged user base into financial results, the company monetizes three ways - via paid content, via advertising, and via IP adaptations.

On the paid content front, the company allows users to subscribe to paywalled premium content put out by creators. Then, for every payment it facilitates, WBTN takes a cut.

On the advertising front, the company sells advertising inventory on its digital properties to marketers.

On the IP Adaptations front, the company takes a cut of content that is licensed from authors and re-created elsewhere into film. See below for an example of this:

Added up, growth in these segments has been mixed, but the company's results have been largely stable over time:

Paid Content makes up a lion's share of the company's business at just over 80% of sales, but advertising and IP have been generally growing as well.

In our view, we're coming into the WBTN story at an interesting time.

On the one hand, YoY growth coming into 2024 hasn't been strong, as the company has instead focused on growing gross margins and making the business more sustainable:

This makes the company appear to be a lower-growth proposition than it otherwise is.

That said, now operating at a more sustainable rate and with solid cash from operations coming into company coffers on a quarterly basis, we think WBTN is poised to begin re-investing in growth and monetization:

Excluding adjustments and other nonrecurring costs, we were pleased with structural improvement in our adjusted EBITDA, which grew to $22.4million, a significant increase from a loss of $0.9 million in the prior year...

At a fundamental level, we were able to improve our profitability by managing costs appropriately to fund our growth initiatives. As a result, our adjusted EPS was $0.20 in the quarter compared to a loss of $0.01 in the prior year period...

We will maintain a balanced capital allocation program that prioritizes long-term value creation for all our stakeholders.

On top of this, the recent IPO has given the company substantial growth capital of more than $400 million to spend on user acquisition.

Anecdotally, we've seen ads on other platforms for WBTN's core properties, which indicates that this is effort has already begun.

This improved financial position and growth capital is primarily why analysts expect significant top and bottom-line growth through 2026:

These estimates appear achievable to us, and we believe that WBTN's long-term outlook, with the engaged user base, fresh capital position, and balanced IRR allocation strategy should create value substantial for shareholders.

WBTN's Valuation

But what are we paying for the stock?

Right now, WBTN is in a bit of a weird spot when it comes to the valuation.

On the top line, WBTN is valued at roughly 0.95x sales and 0.95x book. This low P/B is due to the company's pristine balance sheet, which has a lot of cash and essentially no debt.

At first glance, these multiples seem highly affordable, given the company's highly engaged user base and monetization potential.

However, when looking at it through a different frame, a majority of the company's revenues are earned in foreign currencies that has put a massive headwind on growth. As a result, YoY increases in sales have been meager, and GAAP earnings have also been sparse, which makes comps difficult.

For our part, we think WBTN is a risky story, but one ultimately worth betting on. If the company can re-accelerate sales growth and diversify its business, then the current price seems like a steal.

As you saw from the picture above, analysts are expecting YoY top-line growth of 20%+ through 2026, which would put the multiple at 0.7x.

0.7x for a company growing revenues at a 20%+ YoY rate seems like a fantastic value, especially if EPS can grow over that span to more than $0.80 per share. This would also put the company at around 13x net income, which appears highly attractive.

However, if the company can't meet analyst expectations, then we see some potential downside for shareholders. Yes, 0.95x book is very, very cheap, but on a sales basis, it appears as though the multiple could fall further if growth doesn't materialize.

We could see 30% - 40% multiple compression in the cards if EPS remains breakeven or margins worsen as revenue grows.

That said, on balance, we think the company will be able to meet growth targets, which gives us confidence in rating the company a 'Strong Buy'. The company's network advantages, unique entertainment proposition, and solid constant currency results give us confidence that investors at this price will be rewarded over the long term.

Summary

Ultimately, we think WBTN is a materially under-valued, under-monetized user attention opportunity with significant white space to grow and a passionate and dedicated user base.

As management invests new growth capital to boost user counts and ARPPU, we see significant long-term upside for the stock, even if there are some risks present in the valuation in the event that execution stumbles.

This is a 'show me' story around management's ability to grow, and we're expecting to be wowed.

As a result, we rate WBTN a 'Strong Buy'.

Cheers!

data.title: Nestlé: 2024 Guidance Cut, But Still A Buy
data.description:
Nestlé (OTCPK:NSRGY, OTCPK:NSRGF) presented its 2024 nine-month results on October 17. As a reminder, Nestlé is the world's largest food and beverage player in the developed and emerging markets. Major brands include Nespresso, Purina, Nescafé, KitKat, and Maggi. Our buy rating was supported by 1) the supportive company's capital allocation with a 28 consecutive DPS increase, 2) a valuation discount compared to Nestlé's historical average, and 3) a positive correlation between higher margin and healthier foods, supporting sales and margin MIX. Since we initiated coverage, Nestlé's stock price has been lagging; however, despite lower guidance in Q3, we are taking advantage of this underperformance and continuing to increase our position.

Q3 Results

Nestlé Group Q3 organic sales growth reached a plus +1.9%, signing a minus 140 basis points compared to consensus expectations. Both Real Internal Growth (RIG) and pricing were also below Wall Street analysts' estimates. In Q3 (Fig 1), Nestlé RIG reached +1.3% vs cons. of +2.3%, and price was 0.6% vs. cons. average of +1.0%. The company reported a softer than expected RIG due to 1) softening consumer demand, particularly in the Americas and Europe, 2) lower customer inventory (particularly in the Americas), which resulted in a 60 basis points adverse impact in the Q3 RIG, and 3) consumer hesitancy toward global brands. FX was minus 3.5%, leading to Q3 reported sales of CHF 22.1 billion. As anticipated by our team, Nestlé revised its Fiscal Year 2024 outlook and now guides for organic sales growth of approximately +2%. The company also lowered its operating profit margin to around 17.0%. This is a 30 basis points contraction on a yearly basis. In our previous estimates, we anticipated an EBIT margin of 17.4% (10 basis points higher vs. 2023 numbers).

Fig 1

Fig 2

Why are we positive?

  1. The CEO is trying to restore investor confidence. He communicated a series of organizational changes. This includes a leaner BoD and fewer geographic regions. This aims to lower the company's cost structure and speed up decision-making. Starting in January 2025, Nestlé will merge the LatAm and North America divisions into the Americas zone and unite China's operations within Asia, Africa, and Oceania. All the new zone heads will be located in Switzerland (Vevey). Aside from the reporting changes, Nestlé is also simplifying the coffee brand structure, eliminating duplication and bringing alignment;
  2. The CEO also highlighted that the company will sharpen its focus on consumers to accelerate performance and regain market share. The CEO reiterated the need to reinvest behind brands, specifying that efficiencies will support the necessary growth leverage;
  3. In the Q&A analyst call, the CFO explained that retailers have held back volumes by reducing their inventories, particularly in countries with weaker economies, such as Latin America. That said, Nestlé's internal data points suggest a positive recovery in 2025. Additionally, she reported that new marketing investments will not impact the company's profitability thanks to productivity and efficiency savings. Here at the Lab, we believe the November Capital Market Day will be the right opportunity to disclose the company's transformation towards digitalization. Additionally, managers will report the new company's innovation tech stack directly to the CEO;
  4. Businesswise, Q2 and Q3 growth were positive in a softer environment. More importantly, the Nestlé Health Science division is also back on a supportive growth trajectory.

Fig 3

Adjusting Estimates and Valuation

The Swiss multinational food and beverage company decided to cut its sales and operating margin forecasts as it struggles to regain market share. Following the 9M results, according to our calculation, Nestlé's adjusted EBIT margin will contract by approximately 80 basis points in Q4. We forecast an operating profit margin of 16.5% in the last quarter. Underlying EPS at constant currency development is now expected to be broadly flat compared to a previous mid-single-digit increase. According to our financial assessment, we previously projected a 2024 turnover of CHF 93.3 billion with an EBIT of CHF 16.7 billion. Despite our forward-thinking positive view, we need to align our estimates with the 9M results and the internal company's projection. Therefore, at the constant exchange rates, we now estimate sales of CHF 91.5 billion in 2024. Based on the current FX rate, the company will likely have a minus 4% drag on the Fiscal Year 2024 top-line sales. With a lower EBIT projection of 17%, we reached an operating profit of CHF 15.55 billion. We calculate that the company's updated guidance should lead to a 3% cut to Wall Street consensus estimates in adjusted EBIT and EPS. Wall Street earnings revision might provide better entry points from now on. Our annual EPS falls from CHF 5.05 to CHF 4.72 compared to our previous forecast. This signaled a minus 1.6% contraction compared to last year. We are now modeling a lower EPS growth vs. Nestlé's latest indication.

On the next catalyst, we believe there will be support from Nestlé CMD in November. However, the food giant will provide a 2025 outlook in February next year.

On the valuation front, Nestlé is inexpensive. The company is a market leader with an operating profit of 17% in a depressed market environment and softer consumer demand. In our calculation, the company trades with an EV/EBITDA of 11.5x and a P/E of 18.22x. As already reported, we are more cautious about the Q4 forecast and below Nestlé EPS guidance. That said, the company valuation is well below its 5-year historical average (Fig 4). Thanks to a leading industry margin, this company has a superior ROIC vs. peers and an economic moat. For consistency reasons, our team continues to value the company with an FWD P/E of 21x, and we arrived at a target price of CHF 99.12 per share. This implied a 16.5% upside from the current share price, plus a 3.5% DPS.

Fig 4

Risks

Our team already covered the Nestlé risks section, so we report the following:

  1. Variances in consumer income and confidence. The company's profitability is derived from sales to consumers. Persistent inflation might be decremental;
  2. Higher competition from private label products;
  3. Value destruction M&A;
  4. Loss on FX;
  5. Inability to increase its pricing power;
  6. Higher-for-longer interest rate, which might impact a volume recovery.
  7. Other corporate risks include changes in regulation, cybersecurity, operational execution, and product liabilities;
  8. (New risk). Additionally, we report prolonged weakness in the coffee and pet care categories. On a positive note, the Health Sciences division is recovering;
  9. (New risk). We also derive our Price Target from a P/E multiple. This might vary in the future.

Conclusion

We see support from the CEO/CFO comments. Nestlé's valuation is inexpensive, and considering our lower outlook, we still project a total return of 20%. Our buy rating is then confirmed.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Uber: Impact Of Potential Acquisition Of Expedia
data.description:
I assigned a ‘Strong Buy’ rating to Uber (NYSE:UBER) in April 2024, highlighting their growth of Uber One membership. On October 16th after the market closed, Financial Times reported that Uber is considering to acquire Expedia (EXPE). I acknowledge that if Uber acquires Expedia, it could lead to some near-term weakness in Uber’s stock price. However, I favor Uber’s long-term growth prospects in both Mobility and Delivery market and reiterate a ‘Strong Buy’ rating on Uber.

Potential Acquisition of Expedia

It is reported that Uber has approached advisers in recent months to explore the acquisition of Expedia, which has a market capitalization of $20 billion. Dara Khosrowshahi, Uber’s CEO, was formerly the CEO of Expedia from 2015 to 2017. I think there are both pros and cons to this potential acquisition:

  • As discussed in my previous article, Uber has been developing their super app and focusing on expanding their Uber One membership. The Uber One membership offers benefits such as $0 delivery fee on food and groceries, 5% discount on eligible deliveries and other perks for rides. On the other hand, Expedia’s app can provide booking services for rental cars, flights and hotels. If Uber can consolidate Expedia’s service into its platform, users could access a bunch of services through one single app.
  • Expedia reported an adjusted EBITDA margin of 20.9% in FY23, significantly higher than Uber’s current adjusted EBITDA margin of 14.7%. As such, the potential acquisition could improve Uber’s margin profile immediately.
  • If Uber decides to acquire Expedia, it would be a quite big acquisition, and Uber would likely need to issue shares to finance the deal. As of the end of Q2 FY24, Uber had $6.3 billion in cash and cash equivalents on their balance sheet, which is not sufficient to acquire Expedia. The potential share issuance could dilute the interests of existing Uber shareholders.
  • Expedia has been competing with Booking Holdings (BKNG) in the hotel and flight booking market. As depicted in the chart below, Expedia’s total number of booked room nights is much smaller than Booking’s, which demonstrates that Booking.com is more favored by customers.

Overall, I favor the potential business combination; however, it is a big acquisition and Uber’s stock price might face pressures in the near-term if they decide to issue equities.

Both Uber and Expedia have not confirmed or denied the potential acquisition so far. I think Uber has more than 50% probability of acquiring Expedia for the following reasons:

  • Dara Khosrowshahi is familiar with Expedia, and he has the capacity to persuade both sides to reach a deal.
  • There is no business overlap between Uber and Expedia, and the combination of the two companies would allow Uber to offer a comprehensive range of services including hotel and flight booking, ride-sharing, food and grocery deliveries.
  • The combination could leverage the customer base from both sides. After the deal, Uber could potentially expand its subscriber base for their Uber One Membership.

Recent Result and Outlook

Uber released their Q2 result on August 6th, reporting 21% growth in gross bookings and 17% growth in revenue on a constant currency basis. My key takeaway is their consistent booking growth during the quarter, driven by both delivery and mobility businesses. The Uber One membership has contributed significantly to this growth, representing more than 50% of total gross bookings. It is evident that the Uber One membership has steadily gained popularity in recent quarters.

Without the deal, I continue to estimate the fair value of Uber’s stock price is $103 per share, with the following assumptions:

  • Mobility: As shown in the chart below, Uber continues to lead the ride-share market, while Lyft (LYFT) has struggled to scale their ride-share business. Uber’s mobility will be driven by customers' growth, increased take rate and a rise in trips per customer. Assuming 2% growth in total customers, 3% growth in take rate and 2% growth in trips per customer, I project Uber’s mobility revenue will grow by 18% annually.

  • Delivery: I continue to forecast Uber will grow their Delivery business by 15%, aligned with recent trends. I think Uber One membership could offer their subscribers tremendous benefits for food and grocery delivery, which will further accelerate the growth of Uber’s Delivery business.

As such, I calculate Uber will sustain 15% revenue growth in the near future, gradually decelerating to 12% from FY28 and 10% from FY30 due to the maturity of their mobility business.

Uber has already experienced margin benefits from the scaling of their delivery business. In addition, the company is investing in technologies to lower the cost per transaction and improve operational efficiencies, which could reduce their overall operating expenses. I project 100bps annual margin expansion driven by 60bps from gross profits, 20bps from R&D optimization and 20bps from reduction in sales and marketing.

The WACC is calculated to be 10% assuming: risk-free rate 3.6%; beta 1.5; equity risk premium 7%; cost of debt 5%; equity $11.2 billion; debt $9.5 billion; tax rate 2%. The DCF can be summarized as follows:

Discounting all the future FCF, the fair value is calculated to be $103 per share without factoring in the potential acquisition, as per my estimates.

As I think the potential acquisition is highly likely, I calculate Uber’s valuation assuming Uber will issue 250 million shares to acquire Expedia. My assumptions for this scenario are as follows:

  • The deal will be completed by the end of FY25, as big acquisitions typically require time for regulatory approvals and antitrust clearances.
  • I assume Expedia will grow their revenue by 7%, aligned with their historical trends.
  • The combination of business will immediately improve Uber’s operating margin by 50bps, assuming Expedia sustains their current margin.
  • With the acquisition, Uber could potentially accelerate the growth of Uber One Membership, attracting more subscribers and achieving higher monetization rate. I project Expedia’s current customer base could accelerate Uber’s growth rate by 2%.

I revised the revenue growth and margin expansion assumptions in the new DCF model accordingly.

I continue to use 10% as the discount rate and the new fair value is calculated to be $130 per share if factoring in the deal, as per my estimates.

Key Risks

If Uber decides to acquire Expedia, Uber could potentially face the disruption risk from alternative accommodation platforms such like Airbnb (ABNB). As illustrated in the chart below, Airbnb has experienced rapid growth in recent years, consistently outperforming Expedia’s revenue growth.

While Expedia is competing with Airbnb via their Vrbo franchise, Vrbo is relative smaller compared to Airbnb in terms of the number of active listings. As shown in the chart below, Google search interest for Airbnb has accelerated in recent years.

In addition, as shown in the chart below, the number of job openings has been declining in recent months. A weak job market could adversely affect Uber’s mobility and delivery businesses, as people might travel less and order food less. To address the affordability issue, Uber has been launching some more budget-friendly solutions, such as two-wheelers, three-wheelers and UberX Share.

Conclusion

While it remains unclear whether Uber will acquire Expedia, I think the deal would make strategic sense for Uber, as it could help transform the Uber app into a super app. I reiterate a ‘Strong Buy’ rating with a fair value of $103 per share without the deal and $130 with the deal.

data.title: Andvari Associates Q3 2024 Letter
data.description:
Dear Friends,

For the first nine months of 2024, Andvari is up 20.3% net of fees while the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY) is up 21.9%.i Andvari clients, please refer to your reports for your specific performance and holdings. The table below shows Andvari's composite performance while the chart shows the cumulative gains of a $100,000 investment.

 14.6% -11.1% 25.4% 20.0% 14.5% -20.5% 8.1% 11.0% 10.0%

During the months of July and August, Andvari's net performance began to catch up with the market. This was mostly due to investors rotating out of the largest and best performing stocks in the S&P and into many of our holdings that had been lagging the market.

This is an example of how quickly performance can change without much (or any) fundamental changes in the businesses we own. It reminds of the old Benjamin Graham quote: "In the short run, the market is a voting machine but in the long run, it is a weighing machine." The quote is evergreen, but a bit overused, so I'd like to restate it in my own words. Perhaps a more accurate translation, and definitely less metaphorical, is this: "In the short term, markets reflect the perception of value, but in the long term it reflects actual value."

This is why it is extremely important to judge Andvari's performance over a long time frame, preferably a stretch of ten years where the market can more accurately judge the true growth in intrinsic value of the businesses we choose to own.

ANDVARI HOLDINGS

There is not much of significance to report about any individual holding over the past quarter. Most of the companies we own continue to perform in line with our expectations. However, we will give some brief reminders of why we own some of what we own.

Our three serial acquirers of vertical market software (VMS) companies are Constellation Software (OTCPK:CNSWF), Topicus.com (OTCPK:TOITF), and Lumine Group (OTCPK:LMGIF). This trio acquired another 22 software businesses in just the prior quarter. Given the decentralized nature of all three companies, and their many different business units and sub-business units, a few acquisitions may have flown under our radar. We continue to view this group as a semi-permanent holding still capable of outperforming the market over the long term.

Arthur J. Gallagher (AJG) and Rollins (ROL) are two other serial acquirers in Andvari's client portfolios. Both are some of the largest, and best, businesses in their respective industries. AJG is a leading property and casualty insurance and reinsurance broker. Rollins is home to many of the top brands in the pest service industry in North America.

Importantly, while both AJG and Rollins have large market shares, their respective markets are still highly fragmented. There are thousands of small and medium-sized businesses left for AJG and Rollins to acquire. Gallagher currently has a pipeline of 100 potential acquisitions that represents about $1.4 billion of annualized revenue (compare this to $10.1 billion of revenues for 2023). Andvari believes the pace of acquisitions for both companies can continue for many years to come. Just see below the acquisition track records of both companies since 2014.

NUMBER OF BUSINESSES ACQUIRED

2014

2015

2016

2017

2018

2019

2020

2021

2022

2023

2014 - 2023

Rollins

21

12

34

23

38

31

39

31

23

282

Arthur J. Gallagher

60

44

37

36

44

46

27

36

36

50

416

CREATING VALUE THROUGH M&A

Whether its our collection of VMS companies, Rollins with their pest service businesses, or AJG's insurance brokerage business, they all have highly rational acquisition strategies and proven acquisition track records. Acquisitions have created enormous value for their respective shareholders over the years. There are several components to how they've created value through acquisition. First is the simple value arbitrage of the acquirer buying a business for a lower multiple than which the acquirer trades at in the market. For example, all else equal, if Company A that trades at 20 times earnings buys Company B at 10 times earnings, the acquired earnings of Company B will usually be valued by the market at somewhere near the multiple of Company A.

But the companies we own do not just play the value arbitrage game. Companies like Constellation, Rollins, or AJG position themselves as the "acquirers of choice" in their respective industries. They offer a fair price, but never top dollar. What they offer in exchange is a permanent home for the business and the employees of the seller. Thus, not only can they earn a potentially better return, they are also better able to select for business owners motivated by more than just money. They can select for business owners who care more about the continued growth and success of their business and employees.

Also, when Constellation, Rollins, or AJG acquire a company, the acquired company benefits by becoming part of a larger organization with greater resources. The acquired company has access to extraordinarily honed best practices.

The acquired company can offer more robust career opportunities for their own employees. Given all the above, it's likely the acquired company can more easily grow its profit margins and revenues at a higher pace post acquisition.

Patrick Gallagher, Chairman and CEO of AJG, talks about the benefits a firm receives when they choose to sell to AJG:

"Most of these firms have less than $25 million of annual revenue and have terrific roots in their local communities. So when they sell and join Gallagher, we get revenue and profit. But more importantly, we get new talent and capabilities. Basically, we get their brains. And they get immediate access to our niche experts, our extensive data and analytics and our centers of excellence. And all of our tools and capabilities are on their desk overnight. That creates immediate value for their clients, gives them a terrific new story for prospects, and it offers enhanced career opportunities for their employees." - September 19, 2024

Another benefit of M&A done correctly is that, in some cases, an acquired company can become an additional acquisition vehicle that can grow by making acquisitions of its own. There are several examples of this that have already happened at Constellation Software. It has also happened at Rollins. Jeff Dunn, Co-President of Northwest Exterminating, one of Rollins' brands based in the South, speaks about the experience of being acquired by Rollins:

"When we were acquired back in 2017, we were very much a family-owned business. And the Phillips family sought out Rollins. And Jerry was the leader at that time, and Jerry said, "Trust us. We'll give you autonomy. You guys can be Northwest, we'll provide resources so that you can scale." And 6.5 years later, we've more than tripled our revenue. We've got opportunities for our teammates that we would have never had before. We've done 15 acquisitions since becoming a part of Rollins. So it's been an incredible experience." - May 17, 2024

ANDVARI TAKEAWAY

When it comes to capital allocation, every business has only five choices. It can: (1) reinvest in its business; (2) pay dividends; (3) repurchase shares; (4) pay down debt; or (5) acquire other businesses. That's it. The choices a company makes on each of these determines whether value is created or destroyed. In the case of M&A, when done correctly and in a programmatic way, the long term results can be incredibly additive. Think of a wintry landscape and the difference between someone rolling a snowball on flat ground and someone pushing a snowball down a long hill. One snowball will more quickly and easily gain in size and speed.

Finally, although we're pleased our performance is catching up to the market, and that the market has recently recognized some of the latent value in our holdings, a year or even two years is not enough to judge Andvari's skill in stock picking. It is the long term by which we should be judged, the next ten years and beyond.

As always, I love to hear from clients and anyone else. Please contact me with your thoughts, comments, or questions. Sincerely,

Douglas E. Ott, II

DISCLOSURES AND END NOTES

iAndvari performance represents actual trading performance of all, actual clients beginning on 4/12/13. Performance from 12/31/12 to 4/12/13 is actual performance of proprietary accounts, namely the accounts of Andvari's principal, Douglas Ott. Andvari believes including Ott's performance figures for the first 4 months and 12 days of 2013 is fair as he managed those accounts similarly to Andvari's first clients. All performance, including the initial proprietary period, are net of management fees- assumed to be 1.25% per annum, paid quarterly, as currently advertised-net of brokerage commissions and expenses, time-weighted, and includes all cash and other securities. Performance includes realized and unrealized returns and excludes the effects of taxes on incurred gains or losses. Andvari does not certify the accuracy of these numbers. Performance data quoted represents past performance and does not guarantee future results.

The exchange traded funds (ETFs) are listed as benchmarks and are total return figures and assumes dividends are reinvested. The SPY ETF is based on the S&P 500 Index, which is a float-adjusted, capitalization-weighted index of 500 U.S. large-capitalization stocks representing all major industries. The IWM ETF is based on the Russell 2000 Index, an index of 2,000 U.S. small-cap stocks. It is not possible to invest directly in an index. Because Andvari client portfolios are non-diversified, the performance of each holding will have a greater impact on results and may make them more volatile than a more diversified index. Andvari also engages or may engage in strategies not employed by the S&P 500 or the Russell 2000 including, without limitation, the use of leverage.

One may request a list of all securities mentioned or recommended for the preceding year as of the date of this letter.

You may contact Andvari using the information below. Actual client results may differ from results depicted in this letter. Any investment involves substantial risks, including, but not limited to, pricing volatility, inadequate liquidity, and the loss of principal. Investment strategies managed by Andvari Associates LLC may have a position in the securities or assets discussed in this article. Securities mentioned may not be representative of the Andvari's current or future investments. Andvari may re-evaluate its holdings in any mentioned securities and may buy, sell or cover certain positions without notice.

The discussion of Andvari's investments and investment strategy (including, but not limited to, current investment themes, the portfolio managers' research and investment process, and portfolio characteristics) represents the views and opinions of Andvari's portfolio managers and Andvari Associates LLC, the investment adviser, at the time of this report, and can change without notice.

This document does not in any way constitute an offer or solicitation of an offer to buy or sell any investment, security, or commodity discussed herein or of any of the affiliates of Andvari.

The information contained in this document may include, or incorporate by reference, forward-looking statements, which would include any statements that are not statements of historical fact. Any or all of Andvari's forward-looking assumptions, expectations, projections, intentions or beliefs about future events may turn out to be wrong. These forward-looking statements can be affected by inaccurate assumptions or by known or unknown risks, uncertainties, and other factors, most of which are beyond Andvari's control. Investors should conduct independent due diligence, with assistance from professional financial, legal and tax experts, on all securities, companies, and commodities discussed in this document and develop a stand-alone judgment of the relevant markets prior to making any investment decision.

Original Post

data.title: Retail Sales Up 0.4% In September, Better Than Expected
data.description:
By Jennifer Nash

The Census Bureau's Advance Retail Sales Report for September revealed headline sales were up 0.4% last month. The latest reading was higher than the expected 0.3% monthly growth in consumer spending.

For an inflation-adjusted perspective on retail sales, take a look at our Real Retail Sales commentary.

Here is the introduction from today's report:

Advance estimates of U.S. retail and food services sales for September 2024, adjusted for seasonal variation and holiday and trading-day differences, but not for price changes, were $714.4 billion, an increase of 0.4 percent (±0.5 percent)* from the previous month, and up 1.7 percent (±0.5 percent) from September 2023. Total sales for the July 2024 through September 2024 period were up 2.3 percent (±0.5 percent) from the same period a year ago. The July 2024 to August 2024 percent change was unrevised from up 0.1 percent (±0.2 percent)*.Retail trade sales were up 0.3 percent (±0.5 percent)* from August 2024, and up 1.4 percent (±0.5 percent) from last year. Nonstore retailers were up 7.1 percent (±1.4 percent) from last year, while food services and drinking places were up 3.7 percent (±2.1 percent) from September 2023.

The chart below is a log-scale snapshot of retail sales since the early 1990s. The three exponential regressions through the data help us to evaluate the long-term trend of this key economic indicator.

  1. The red line is a linear regression through the complete data series.
  2. The green line is a regression from the start of the series through the end of 2007 and then extrapolated to the present - thus excluding the Financial Crisis.
  3. The blue line is a regression from the start of the series through the end of 2019 and then extrapolated to the present - thus excluding the COVID-19 pandemic.

Monthly retail sales have been above the red and blue line since March 2021, signaling increased consumer spending that was most likely pent-up as a result of the pandemic.

The year-over-year percent change provides another perspective on the historical trend. Current retail sales are up 1.7% compared to September 2023. Here is the headline series with a callout to the most recent 12 months.

Core Retail Sales

Core sales (ex Autos) rose 0.5% in September, coming in higher than the expected 0.1% growth.

Core retail sales are up 2.2% compared to September 2023. Here is the year-over-year chart of core retail sales with a callout to the most recent 12 months.

Retail Sales: “Control” Purchases

The next two charts illustrate retail sales “control” purchases, which is an even more “core” view of retail sales. This series excludes motor vehicles & parts, gasoline, building materials as well as food services & drinking places.

The popular financial press typically ignores this series, but it's a more consistent and reliable reading of the economy. Retail sales control purchases were up 0.7% in September.

Similar to the retail sales snapshot chart earlier, the chart below is a log-scale snapshot of control purchases since the early 1990s and includes two of the exponential regressions previously mentioned.

Here is the same series year-over-year. Current control purchases are up 4% compared to September 2023.

For a better sense of the reduced volatility of the “control” series, here is a YoY overlay with the headline retail sales. Note that the two series follow each other closely, but headline sales have more extreme highs and lows than the control series.

Retail sales will impact interest in the SPDR S&P Retail ETF (XRT), VanEck Retail ETF (RTH), Amplify Online Retail ETF (IBUY), and ProShares Online Retail ETF (ONLN).

Original Post

Editor's Note: The summary bullets for this article were chosen by Seeking Alpha editors.
